task_summary: Add best-effort external linked media extraction to TopicEditor so that Reddit, Twitter/X, and similar URLs found in source messages or embeds are resolved, downloaded (with strict safeguards), cached, and exposed as media_refs for the agent to bind to document blocks. The publisher interleaves resolved media with block text and falls back to the original link on failure — external extraction failure never blocks text publishing.
key_evidence: media_refs currently only support kind='attachment' or kind='embed' — normalize_media_ref rejects anything else (topic_editor.py:3412-3415)
_message_payload already extracts media_urls and media_refs_available but only from Discord attachments/embeds, not from message content text (topic_editor.py:3056-3110)
_first_available_media_ref_for_sources only scans Discord attachments/embeds — no external URL scanning (topic_editor.py:1894-1920)
_resolve_media_url_from_metadata only resolves from attachments[] and embeds[] top-level — no external resolver path (topic_editor.py:3703-3728)
render_topic_publish_units interleaves text blocks and media units, calling _resolve_media_url_from_metadata per ref — this is the publish-time resolution point (topic_editor.py:3597-3700)
_publish_topic sends flat_messages as Discord messages, treating media URLs as plain text messages (channel.send) — no file attachment upload path currently (topic_editor.py:2794-2806)
Embed URL handling already exists for Discord link previews: embeds can contain url/thumbnail/image/video fields (topic_editor.py:3074-3097)

Curator module (curator.py:133-145) maintains a list of 'allowed domains' including fixupx.com, fxtwitter.com, vxtwitter.com, rxddit.com — these are embed-fix domains for Twitter and Reddit, confirming these are the target external platforms
The vision_clients module (src/common/vision_clients.py) provides _sha256 and download patterns (requests.get with timeout) — same pattern should be reused
_dispatch_understand_media demonstrates a 3-tier cache: PK cache → hash cache → API call with budget — this pattern should be replicated for external media
No yt-dlp, youtube-dl, or similar dependency exists in the project — needs to be added
The auto-shortlist system (_auto_shortlist_media_messages) only shortlists messages with Discord attachments/embeds — external-media-bearing messages with high reactions are invisible to it
relevant_code: 1. src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py:3056-3110 — _message_payload: where media_urls and media_refs_available are built; needs external URL detection
2. src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py:809-851 — _build_initial_user_payload: builds the source payload for the agent; needs external media refs in source_messages
3. src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py:3387-3423 — normalize_media_ref: validates kind must be 'attachment' or 'embed'; needs to accept 'external'
4. src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py:3703-3728 — _resolve_media_url_from_metadata: resolves URLs from attachments/embeds; needs external resolver path
5. src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py:3597-3700 — render_topic_publish_units: interleaves text+media; already handles url=None gracefully (skips media unit)
6. src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py:2720-2831 — _publish_topic (structured path): sends flat_messages as Discord text; media URLs sent as plain messages — may need file-attachment path
7. src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py:1894-1920 — _first_available_media_ref_for_sources: fallback media ref from Discord attachments/embeds only
9. src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py:1922-2088 — _auto_shortlist_media_messages: shortlists by reaction count + Discord media; could be extended for external URL messages
10. src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py:1552-1861 — _dispatch_create_topic_tool: validates media_refs per block; needs to handle 'external' kind
11. src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py:394-421 — TopicEditor.__init__: configuration/env vars are set here; new env vars for external resolver go here
12. src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py:853-907 — _enrich_media_understandings: caches vision understandings per attachment; analogous external understanding enrichment needed
13. src/common/vision_clients.py:1-438 — Existing vision client module with _sha256, download patterns, retry logic — patterns to reuse for external media download
14. src/features/curating/curator.py:133-145 — Allowed external embed domains list (fixupx.com, rxddit.com, etc.) — confirms target platforms
15. tests/test_topic_editor_core.py:480-521 — TestResolveMediaUrlFromMetadata: existing tests for _resolve_media_url_from_metadata; needs external ref tests
16. tests/test_topic_editor_core.py:33-76 — TestNormalizeMediaRef: existing tests for normalize_media_ref; needs external kind tests
17. tests/test_topic_editor_media_understanding.py — Test patterns for cache hits, budget, enrichment; external resolver should follow same patterns
18. tests/test_topic_editor_runtime.py — Runtime test patterns with FakeDB; new FakeDB methods needed for external cache
test_expectations: test_normalize_media_ref_accepts_external_kind — normalize_media_ref should accept kind='external' with url field
test_external_url_detection_in_message_content — _message_payload should detect Reddit/Twitter URLs in content text
test_external_url_detection_in_embeds — _message_payload should detect external URLs in embed url fields
test_resolve_external_media_url — _resolve_media_url_from_metadata should return cached file path for external refs
test_external_resolver_reddit_url — resolver extracts media from a Reddit-like URL (yt-dlp metadata mode)
test_external_resolver_twitter_url — resolver extracts media from a Twitter/X-like URL
test_external_resolver_failure_fallback — when resolver fails, media_ref still surfaces but publish falls back to original link
test_external_resolver_cache_hit — second request for same URL returns cached result without re-download
test_external_resolver_oversize_media — media exceeding size limit is skipped with log, not a crash
test_external_resolver_timeout — download timeout produces log + fallback, not crash
test_publisher_fallback_to_original_link — when external media can't be resolved, publish unit includes original link
test_publisher_interleaves_external_media — external media units appear after their block text in correct order
test_external_extraction_failure_does_not_block_text — text blocks publish even when external media resolution fails
constraints: Never make external extraction failure block text publishing — text always goes through
Prefer archived Discord attachments/embeds first; external URLs are best-effort secondary
Strict timeout, file-size, content-type, and domain safelist on downloads
Cache resolved results so repeated runs do not re-download (use content-hash + URL key)
Keep secrets out of logs (URLs in logs should be sanitized if they contain tokens)
Do not reintroduce direct auto-posting at five reactions — the auto-shortlist-to-watching flow stays
Must not break existing attachment/embed media_ref resolution
All new functions must be pure/testable — follow existing stateless pattern of normalize_media_ref / _resolve_media_url_from_metadata
yt-dlp should be called in metadata-only mode first (--dump-json) to get direct media URLs before downloading
Domain safelist: only allow known media-sharing domains (Reddit, Twitter/X, Instagram, TikTok, etc.) — match curator's allowed_domains list as starting point
primary_criterion: The agent can reference external media (from Reddit/Twitter URLs in source messages) via structured media_refs in blocks, and the publisher interleaves resolved media files with text blocks — with extraction failures gracefully falling back to original links without blocking text publication.
suggested_approach: 1. Add a new module src/features/summarising/external_media_resolver.py with: URL detection (regex for Reddit, Twitter/X, etc.), yt-dlp metadata download with strict timeout/size/type safeguards, content-hash-based caching via db handler, and domain safelist. 2. Extend normalize_media_ref to accept kind='external' with {message_id, kind:'external', url, content_hash?, resolved_path?}. 3. Modify _message_payload to detect external URLs in message.content and embeds, enrich media_refs_available with external entries. 4. Add _resolve_external_media or extend _resolve_media_url_from_metadata to handle kind='external' — returns cached file path or falls back to original URL. 5. Modify _publish_topic to handle file uploads for resolved external media (discord.File) instead of sending URLs as plain text. 6. Add cache table/getter/upserter to db_handler for external_media_cache (keyed by URL hash). 7. Make all extraction best-effort: log resolved/downloaded/skipped/fallback cases, never raise on extraction failure. 8. Add comprehensive tests following existing test patterns in test_topic_editor_core.py and test_topic_editor_media_understanding.py.
